perturbation.original_text: Client agrees to pay to Company the sum of $5,000 (the "Contract Price") to design and develop a website for Client (the "Client Website") in accordance with the accompanying Scope of Work, attached to this Agreement as Exhibit A. (a) Change Orders. Any changes to the Scope of Work following the execution of this Agreement requiring Additional Work must be submitted to and accepted by Company in writing as a "Change Order". The costs of any such changes shall be added to the Contract Price.
perturbation.changed_text: Client agrees to pay to Company the sum of $5,000 (the "Contract Price") to design and develop a website for Client (the "Client Website"). (a) Change Orders. Any changes to the Scope of Work following the execution of this Agreement requiring Additional Work must be submitted to and accepted by Company in writing as a "Change Order". The costs of any such changes may be added to the Contract Price at the sole discretion of the company.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause stated that any changes to the scope of work will have their costs added to the contract price. The modified clause introduces uncertainty by saying that the costs *may* be added *at the sole discretion of the company*. This contradicts the initial agreement that the client would pay an additional sum for changes made to the scope of work, by now giving the company the power to decide whether to add the costs or not.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Client agrees to pay to Company the sum of $5,000 (the "Contract Price") to design and develop a website for Client (the "Client Website"). (a) Change Orders. Any changes to the Scope of Work following the execution of this Agreement requiring Additional Work must be submitted to and accepted by Company in writing as a "Change Order". The costs of any such changes may be added to the Contract Price at the sole discretion of the company. Additional Work shall be defined as the addition or revision of pages, graphics, or other features, any text, graphic or page design or programming requiring more than two rounds of textual or graphical revisions, substantial revisions to text and/or content provided by Client, changes to elements which have been finalized, or significant changes in the Scope of Work.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction by altering the original agreement regarding payment for changes to the scope of work. Initially, the original text stated that the costs of changes *shall* be added to the contract price. The changed text modifies this to state that the costs *may* be added at the company's discretion, directly contradicting the original obligation and creating uncertainty. Since the Changed Text and Contradicted text are the same, but the Change Text is what caused a change in meaning with the original text, there exists an In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Company will use its best efforts to deliver the Client Website in the time frame specified in the Scope of Work. Terms of the project: 12 weeks from February 8, 2018 to May 3, 2018. Client acknowledges and agrees that any due dates set forth in the Scope of Work are subject to delay if Company does not receive the required materials or documentation in a timely manner or in the required format or if approvals are delayed by Client or if the Scope of Work is changed by Client.
perturbation.changed_text: Company will use its best efforts to deliver the Client Website in the time frame specified in the Scope of Work. Client acknowledges and agrees that the delivery date of the Client Website is at the sole discretion of the Company. Client acknowledges and agrees that any due dates set forth in the Scope of Work are subject to delay if Company does not receive the required materials or documentation in a timely manner or in the required format or if approvals are delayed by Client or if the Scope of Work is changed by Client.
perturbation.explanation: Originally, the contract specified a timeframe for delivery (12 weeks) and mentioned that delays could occur only under specific circumstances involving Client actions (failure to provide materials, delayed approvals, scope changes). The modified version introduces a clause stating that the 'delivery date... is at the sole discretion of the Company'. This directly contradicts the initial timeframe by allowing the company to change the delivery date for any reason or no reason, removing the constraint of the 12-week period and the specific delay conditions. This introduces uncertainty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Company will use its best efforts to deliver the Client Website in the time frame specified in the Scope of Work. Client acknowledges and agrees that the delivery date of the Client Website is at the sole discretion of the Company. Client acknowledges and agrees that any due dates set forth in the Scope of Work are subject to delay if Company does not receive the required materials or documentation in a timely manner or in the required format or if approvals are delayed by Client or if the Scope of Work is changed by Client.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction. Originally, a 12-week timeframe was specified for project delivery, subject to delays only under specific conditions related to the client's actions. The modified text states that the 'delivery date is at the sole discretion of the Company.' This directly conflicts with the original timeframe, effectively nullifying it. The contradicted text, which is identical to the changed text in this case, now presents an inconsistency. The two clauses together create uncertainty and potential unenforceability, making it unclear whether the 12-week timeframe is binding or whether the company can change the delivery date at will. This fits the definition of a structural flaw causing an in-text contradiction, as a clause is placed in multiple locations (implicitly the original agreement and this new modification) with conflicting implications.

perturbation.original_text: Client shall make a prepayment of $1,900 and pay the remaining $3,100 on completion of the Scope of Work.
perturbation.changed_text: Client shall make a prepayment of $1,900 and pay the remaining $3,100 subject to the Company’s sole discretion of approval upon subjective satisfaction of the Scope of Work completion.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause mandates the remaining payment of $3,100 upon completion of the Scope of Work. The modified clause introduces a condition where the payment is now 'subject to the Company’s sole discretion of approval upon subjective satisfaction'. This contradicts the original agreement by making the payment dependent on the Company's subjective opinion rather than the objective completion of the work, adding uncertainty on payment.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Upon the signing of this Agreement, Client agrees to pay to Company a total of $5,000. Client shall make a prepayment of $1,900 and pay the remaining $3,100 subject to the Company’s sole discretion of approval upon subjective satisfaction of the Scope of Work completion.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a subjective condition ('subject to the Company’s sole discretion of approval upon subjective satisfaction') for the payment of the remaining $3,100. The contradicted text, 'Client shall make a prepayment of $1,900 and pay the remaining $3,100 subject to the Company’s sole discretion of approval upon subjective satisfaction of the Scope of Work completion,' shows that the payment obligation is now dependent on the Company's subjective opinion, contradicting the initial agreement of a fixed payment upon completion. This makes enforcement unclear, since subjective satisfaction is subject to interpretation and challenges, whereas payment upon 'completion' is more objectively verifiable.
